tasks.subject: Task 1: Remove --allow-cross-fs and the samefs check
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Delete the cross-filesystem guard around source `.scram` auto-delete. Pack always removes `<scram>` after a verified pack (unless `--keep-source`), regardless of filesystem.

**Files:**
- Modify: `main.go` (drop flag, drop `maybeRemoveSource` helper, inline `os.Remove`)
- Modify: `help.go` (drop `--allow-cross-fs` lines from pack help)
- Delete: `samefs_unix.go`
- Delete: `samefs_windows.go`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `miniscram pack --allow-cross-fs disc.cue` exits non-zero with "flag provided but not defined: -allow-cross-fs"
- [ ] No file in the repo references `sameFilesystem`, `maybeRemoveSource`, `allowCrossFS`, or `--allow-cross-fs`
- [ ] `go test ./...` passes
- [ ] `go vet ./...` clean

**Verify:** `go build ./... && go test ./... && grep -r 'sameFilesystem\|allowCrossFS\|allow-cross-fs\|maybeRemoveSource' . --include='*.go' --include='*.md' | grep -v 'docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-2'` → no output

**Steps:**
- Delete `samefs_unix.go` and `samefs_windows.go`
- `main.go`: drop `allowCrossFS` flag declaration in `runPack`
- `main.go`: replace `maybeRemoveSource(...)` call with inline `os.Remove(scramPath)` + reporter messages
- `main.go`: delete the `maybeRemoveSource` helper function
- `help.go`: drop the two `--allow-cross-fs` lines from `packHelpText`
- Build, vet, test, grep-verify, commit

```json:metadata
{"files": ["main.go", "help.go", "samefs_unix.go", "samefs_windows.go"], "verifyCommand": "go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test ./...", "acceptanceCriteria": ["--allow-cross-fs flag rejected", "no residual references in code/markdown (excl. historical specs)", "go test passes", "go vet clean"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 2: Remove --no-verify from pack and unpack
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Remove the `--no-verify` flag from both `pack` and `unpack`. Pack always verifies. Unpack always hashes the output. Internal `UnpackOptions.Verify` survives so the `Verify` subcommand can still suppress Unpack's redundant hash compare; `SuppressVerifyWarning` becomes dead and goes.

**Files:**
- Modify: `main.go` (drop flags from `runPack` and `runUnpack`, simplify call sites)
- Modify: `pack.go` (drop `Verify` field from `PackOptions`, drop skip-branch, update comment)
- Modify: `unpack.go` (drop the warn block, drop `SuppressVerifyWarning` field)
- Modify: `help.go` (drop both `--no-verify` lines)
- Modify: `README.md` (drop `[--no-verify]` from both USAGE lines)
- Modify (tests): drop `Verify: true` from 7 `PackOptions` literals: `pack_test.go:21`, `verify_test.go:23`, `cli_test.go:147`, `cli_test.go:182`, `e2e_test.go:58`, `e2e_test.go:110`, `e2e_redump_test.go:120`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `miniscram pack --no-verify disc.cue` exits non-zero with "flag provided but not defined: -no-verify"
- [ ] `miniscram unpack --no-verify disc.miniscram` exits non-zero with "flag provided but not defined: -no-verify"
- [ ] No file references `noVerify`, `--no-verify`, `SuppressVerifyWarning`, or `PackOptions.Verify`
- [ ] `UnpackOptions.Verify` still exists (used internally by `Verify` subcommand)
- [ ] `go test ./...` passes
- [ ] `go vet ./...` clean

**Verify:** `go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test ./...` → all pass; `grep -rn 'no-verify\|noVerify\|SuppressVerifyWarning' . --include='*.go' --include='*.md' | grep -v 'docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04'` → no output

**Steps:**
- `pack.go`: drop `Verify` field from `PackOptions`
- `pack.go`: drop the `if !opts.Verify { warn; return nil }` skip-branch and update the leading comment
- `unpack.go`: drop `SuppressVerifyWarning` field from `UnpackOptions`
- `unpack.go`: replace the inner warn block — keep `if !opts.Verify { return nil }` early-return
- `unpack.go`: drop `SuppressVerifyWarning: true` from the `Verify` subcommand's `Unpack(...)` call
- `main.go`: drop `noVerify` from `runPack` flag set, drop `noVerifyImpliesKeep` block, drop `Verify: !noVerify` keyword from the `Pack(...)` call
- `main.go`: drop `noVerify` from `runUnpack` flag set, set `Verify: true` in the `Unpack(...)` call
- `help.go`: drop both `--no-verify` lines (pack help + unpack help)
- `README.md`: drop `[--no-verify]` from both USAGE lines
- Test files: drop `Verify: true` from 7 `PackOptions` literals (do NOT touch `UnpackOptions.Verify` literals)
- Build, vet, test, smoke-check rejection of removed flags, grep-verify, commit

```json:metadata
{"files": ["main.go", "pack.go", "unpack.go", "help.go", "README.md", "pack_test.go", "verify_test.go", "cli_test.go", "e2e_test.go", "e2e_redump_test.go"], "verifyCommand": "go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test ./...", "acceptanceCriteria": ["pack/unpack reject --no-verify", "no residual references in code/markdown (excl. historical specs)", "UnpackOptions.Verify field still present", "go test passes", "go vet clean"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 3: Tidy inspect --full help wording
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Drop the "(no cap)" idiom from `inspect --full`'s help text. The phrase reads as genz slang ("no cap" = "no lie") and is redundant with "every override."

**Files:**
- Modify: `help.go` (`inspectHelpText`)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `miniscram inspect --help` no longer shows "(no cap)"
- [ ] `go test ./...` passes

**Verify:** `go build ./... && go test ./... && grep -n 'no cap' help.go` → no match in help.go

**Steps:**
- `help.go`: change the `--full` block to drop "(no cap)." and tidy line wrapping
- Confirm no snapshot test references the string
- Build, test, commit

```json:metadata
{"files": ["help.go"], "verifyCommand": "go build ./... && go test ./...", "acceptanceCriteria": ["no 'no cap' in help.go", "go test passes"]}
```
